name: John Hickenlooper
subject: I hope you know
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
|
#
Folks, we're so close to reaching our $250,000 online goal tonight that I'm hoping your donation could put us over the edge. This is our last fundraising goal before our big FEC deadline in a few weeks. If we miss this, we'll be off track for the entire quarter -- and now that the GOP has launched their first set of TV attack ads against me, we cannot afford to fall short. **So, how about it? Can you help us out by pitching in a donation before midnight to help us reach our critical mid-month goal? name: Rep. Katie Porter
subject: I'm a top target for national Republicans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Voter Protection Project and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
---
| | Peter -- from the moment I took office, I've been one of the top targets for national Republicans in 2020. **Now,_Axios_ reports that President Trump is "personally invested" in taking back the House, and is planning a "revenge tour" as payback for impeachment.** I didn't support impeachment to hurt Trump's fragile ego. I did it because of the oath I took to defend the Constitution! But with a cruel president out for vengeance, we'll need to work even harder to hold this seat -- and keep the House blue. Not me. I won't take a dime of corporate PAC money. Together, we won in 2018 by running a people-powered campaign -- and that's how we'll beat Trump and the GOP in 2020. | | | [No thanks, I'll stick with Trump](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001bDS6XDmeZhE6UJpC64ITaBTgZDxy3u-ixDR1wl6FsNdVziUMgHHtSWbLeNDLo2VFIDNzVlieY30nQ-Ud6l8g6BryAFzYSCv5PGzoSnoeQVmKOabiQa8Jc0Qhk39CRZbVnV8cJud6H34dwlMzBpgKpCVX7hH12ATDJYOEstRFgXTsE6jvJfJfXg==&c=4cpLlDoMmpWURgnAmtHM3SED8BZhltTzGCVbchIpdhJBvDiKVosUrg==&ch=Pkwy0qbF2doSCawUKyMaoylPZI2F9W3O8kbu9IdLb2e5fmDQEemYAA==)

name: Dan Lipinski
subject: Election Night Event Cancelled
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LIPINSKI FOR CONGRESS
body:
Dear Peter:

In light of the public health crisis we face regarding the spread of COVID-19 and after careful consideration, the Congressman’s election night event is being cancelled.

This was an extremely difficult decision. After a long and hard-fought campaign, we were looking forward to an exciting election night victory celebration to thank all our friends and supporters, but this crisis requires all of us to strictly adhere to the public health guidelines that have been provided by medical professionals.

Please remember that you can early vote throughout the day today and of course, on election day tomorrow. Team Lipinski will be running strong until the polls close tomorrow, so please stay in touch if you would like to make phone calls. And of course, any donation will help us cross the finish line with a big victory.

As always, thank you for your support. Be safe and take care of each other.

Team Lipinski

subject: We can beat this vulnerable Republican
disclaimer_text: Paid for by DePasquale for PA 10

| Friends, After much thought, and in the abundance of caution, we have decided to temporarily postpone all public events hosted by my campaign. This will include all scheduled house parties and fundraisers, including my Hampton Fundraiser currently scheduled for Monday, March 23. To all of those who have already RSVP'd for these events, we will let you know once they have been rescheduled. I think it is important that we take the advice of President Trump and Governor Sununu and remain calm, but cautious about the potential spread of Covid-19. Please direct yourself to the [state's website here](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xMzc4MjYzMjEwNjg3MjA3MzkwJmM9aDhqNSZlPTE5MjgmYj0zMjg0NDgzODYmZD16MXMzeTFx.MbRC_6AKZ3rD3l9n8Kbd83aymREkvCB0JUO680h9tDo) with any questions about health and prevention. While we are currently postponing public events, that does not mean that our campaign will be taking one minute off! We will be taking full advantage of social media platforms and other technology to to keep our supporters connected and get our message out to the Granite State. If you have not already done so, please follow me on [Facebook](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xMzc4MjYzMjEwNjg3MjA3MzkwJmM9aDhqNSZlPTE5MjgmYj0zMjg0NDgzOTAmZD1vNmE3bDhx.y0D6A-1H4jaGjJnRW8NbjpoC1976u9nNZOkBG0yqBGI) and [Twitter](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xMzc4MjYzMjEwNjg3MjA3MzkwJmM9aDhqNSZlPTE5MjgmYj0zMjg0NDgzOTImZD13Nmw2bjJ4.OUKkWeAOA8JjswQNboqE-6xyxWbWgUaiofDwm1sxzs4) and [if you would like to make a donation to my campaign, you can do so online](https://click.ml.mailersend.com/link/c/YT0xMzc4MjYzMjEwNjg3MjA3MzkwJmM9aDhqNSZlPTE5MjgmYj0zMjg0NDgzOTQmZD1zOGY0cjlv.u8HPgvqvDsjF_22dWDZruRDsJwgF0tig7G2wsmsEieY). I also would encourage anyone that would like get in touch with me and discuss the campaign to give me a call on my cell phone at [603-969-7077](tel:603-969-7077) or email [me at mattmayberrynh@gmail.com. ](mailto:me at mattmayberrynh@gmail.com. ) I hope that you all remain safe, and watch out for friends and neighbors in need. God Bless! \- Matt

subject: TONIGHT - Talks with Docs about the Coronavirus

| |

__Join us on Facebook Live tonight for__ __Talks with Docs: What Science Says__ _Hello Peter,_ During this difficult time, we would like to help share important public health information and provide you with an opportunity to ask questions to a variety of physician experts in the Cleveland area. To that end, we will be featuring physician interviews through Facebook Live on my Facebook page [@BetsyRaderOH](https://act.myngp.com/el/s2SN7MOg1ipXw-1ohUCJxARbyFGi7rZcA0QmUXfTHFc=/-cPMommWdXdakoSBt3j5m9n4AfCT9i4g0ixhvJ_tP5E=). We held one last Thursday and our next one is TONIGHT at 8:00 p.m. **Please join us and share the word about**** _ Talks with Docs: What Science Says_ with physicians Dr. Arthur Lavin and Dr. Michael Lederman** **who will discuss individual and public health responses to the current coronavirus pandemic.** [](https://act.myngp.com/el/s2SN7MOg1ipXw-1ohUCJxARbyFGi7rZcA0QmUXfTHFc=/-cPMommWdXdakoSBt3j5m9n4AfCT9i4g0ixhvJ_tP5E=) Dr. Lavin and Dr. Lederman are leaders of Doctors Organized for Health Care Solutions, which includes over 400 doctors from every major medical center in the Cleveland area. Dr. Lederman is the Scott R. Inkley Professor of Medicine and Professor of Microbiology/Molecular Biology, Pathology and Biomedical Ethics at Case Western Reserve University. He is the editor in chief of a highly regarded international journal on infectious diseases. Dr. Lavin is one of the top pediatricians in the country and serves as the Chair of the American Academy of Pediatrics Committee on Psychosocial Aspects of Child and Family Health. He trained at Harvard University, The Ohio State University and MIT. **Please [click here](https://act.myngp.com/el/s2SN7MOg1ipXw-1ohUCJxARbyFGi7rZcA0QmUXfTHFc=/V2em971NI_v79vxziZwb6coXJFJFmQs-zxs2gR_2y7g=) or go to [Betsy Rader for Ohio](https://act.myngp.com/el/s2SN7MOg1ipXw-1ohUCJxARbyFGi7rZcA0QmUXfTHFc=/wFTqO8ZtG-UGkFjAWKvIKTacujH7HF5xd9xkpNtytGA=)**** on Facebook** **at ****8 pm Monday March 16 (TONIGHT!) to join.** Like and Share the post so we can grow the conversation. We look forward to seeing you online and helping to disseminate useful science-based information about the important issues facing our country. Sincerely, _P.S. You can[click here ](https://act.myngp.com/el/s2SN7MOg1ipXw-1ohUCJxARbyFGi7rZcA0QmUXfTHFc=/Sn3innukUUlFD-kgn56-kwsoi-5Ffz7L4GDbfm5W9ME=)to watch my recent Facebook Live with Dr. Lavin, regarding responses to the coronavirus pandemic._

Dear Peter:

In an editorial yesterday, the Chicago Tribune newspaper made a strong case for getting out to vote in these trying times, and they also offered very strong words of support for Dan:

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP05/1/70880/images/TribuneLogo.png]

Put Lipinski on the ballot in November
“U.S. Rep. Dan Lipinski of Western Springs, one of the few conservative, pro-life Democrats on Capitol Hill, has been a good fit for the 3rd Congressional District for a long time. He’s seeking his ninth term, but to get there he needs to defeat three challengers, including progressive Marie Newman — again. Lipinski barely defeated her in the 2018 primary. Lipinski still has his workhorse energy, which benefits all of his constituents. He’s a moderate on most issues with a bipartisan outlook that’s a welcome balance to Washington’s conservative-progressive divide. Newman, who leans far more left, supports massively expensive and unworkable government spending notions such as the Green New Deal. Taxpayers, put Lipinski on the November ballot.”

Now more than ever, we need leaders like Dan who will fight for our communities with reasoned, commonsense leadership. Please get out and cast your vote, and as the Tribune stated, “be careful, be sensible, be fast.”

God Bless,
Team Lipinski

subject: Trump’s Coronavirus National Emergency Declaration Allows US to Escalate War on Virus
body:
“History condemns generals who plan to fight the last war, rather than anticipate the challenges ahead.” | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c9f3e361fd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Trump’s Coronavirus National Emergency Declaration Allows US to Escalate War on Virus**

“History condemns generals who plan to fight the last war, rather than anticipate the challenges ahead. Effective leaders strive to get in front of the problem,” the Heritage Foundation’s James Jay Carafano writes for Fox News.

“In his Rose Garden news conference Friday outlining the federal government’s next steps to get in front of the advancing coronavirus pandemic, President Trump was much more forward-looking than his critics.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=68e8bd8f27&e=3ee4985eea)

**MORE:** ['Moved quickly': Gov. Cuomo praises President Trump's Coronavirus response](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c01a71dac5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| President Trump called out false reporting on Sunday about “a nationwide Wuhan coronavirus website that Google is creating in partnership with the federal government. Several news outlets erroneously accused the president of lying and misleading the public about the existence and scope of the project,” Bronson Stocking reports for [_Townhall_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d1b8d936cd&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| The Trump Administration’s proposal to waive the payroll tax is the best way to provide Americans with financial relief. “Members of Congress should put aside their partisan and ideological differences now and immediately pass this strategic initiative, or American workers and businesses will bear the brunt of any coronavirus economic pain,” White House Trade & Manufacturing Policy Director Peter Navarro writes for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e131a5265a&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| FACT CHECK: Critics of President Trump falsely claimed that “the Trump administration gutted the National Security Council counter-pandemics effort. It did not. While the Trump administration has rightly reorganized the NSC away from the bureaucratic behemoth it became under the Obama administration, NSC bio-defense efforts have continued,” Tom Rogan writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=829d3c66d4&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|

body:

Peter,

The coronavirus pandemic is certainly frightening.

We all have concerns for our own and loved ones’ health and wellbeing during this time -- but there are steps we can take together to lessen the risk of spreading the virus.

By following these precautions set out by the **[Centers for Disease Control and Prevention](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16162949/171380477/2080337457?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGMwMzE5MS1jMzY3LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kOBf57jBkm23qtuaLR9JCuvHeR92g6mxMZe7LpMYFDY=&emci=741b1f0d-ad67-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=a8c03191-c367-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6658013)** , we can reduce the chances of spreading and contracting coronavirus:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16162950/171380478/2080337457?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGMwMzE5MS1jMzY3LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kOBf57jBkm23qtuaLR9JCuvHeR92g6mxMZe7LpMYFDY=&emci=741b1f0d-ad67-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=a8c03191-c367-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6658013)
---

It’s on each and every one of us to follow the procedures set out by medical professionals at this time -- we need to keep each other and ourselves safe.

**[Visit the Iowa Department of Public Health’s website for information during this time.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16162951/171380479/-12495567?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOGMwMzE5MS1jMzY3LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kOBf57jBkm23qtuaLR9JCuvHeR92g6mxMZe7LpMYFDY=&emci=741b1f0d-ad67-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=a8c03191-c367-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6658013)**

While we might see only a few cases in Iowa for the time being, we should still follow health and safety measures to mitigate the risk of coronavirus spreading among our communities.

Stay safe, Iowa. We are resilient, and we’ll make it through this together.

\-- Team Scholten

This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Have questions, concerns, or ideas? Simply reply to this email — we love hearing from supporters! name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Coronavirus: Latest Indiana Updates
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
As we continue to learn more about the coronavirus (COVID-19), we want to ensure that you have the most recent updates to keep you informed. | | |
---|---|---

Friend,

This week, Governor Holcomb is continuing to take actions to protect Hoosiers from the global coronavirus outbreak – and we wanted to provide you with the latest updates.

**Here are actions Governor Holcomb is taking today:**

* Indiana will adhere to the CDC’s guidance for large events and gatherings, which recommend **no in-person events of more than 50 people.**
* **273 public schools in Indiana are currently closed,** using e-learning days, or are on spring break with future closings planned under current state guidelines. The Indiana Department of Education is working with the remaining 16 school corporations on next steps.
* **Indiana’s restaurants, bars and nightclubs will close to in-person guests** – with take-out and delivery options available through the end of the month.
* **Hospitals and ambulatory surgical centers should postpone elective and non-urgent surgeries** immediately to conserve the resources of our healthcare system. Critical procedures should continue.
* The state’s **Emergency Operations Center has been raised to a Level 1 status** and will work in conjunction with the incident command center at the Indiana State Department of Health for planning, coordination, predictive analysis and other functions.
* State employees should **maximize the use of remote work white still maintaining operations.** State agencies already are developing remote work plans for employees and will continue to implement them while maintaining necessary state services. Non-essential meetings will be limited to 10 people, while high-risk individuals should not attend meetings in person.
* State employees over the age of 60 with underlying health conditions should **work from home,** and agencies should identify work that these individuals can complete remotely.
* **The Indiana State Museum and Historic Sites will be closed,** as will the White River State Park visitors center.
* **Indiana’s state parks and recreations centers will remain open,** including park inns. Restaurants will convert operations to take-out and delivery.
* The Department of Workforce Development (DWD) will **request flexibility under federal and state law to expand eligibility for Hoosiers claiming benefits and to ease burdens on employers.** The DWD will also suspend rules requiring certain unemployment insurance claimants to physically appear at a Work One location to engage in reemployment services for the next four weeks.
* The Indiana Economic Development Corporation will **postpone the inaugural Indiana Global Economic Summit,** scheduled for April 26-28.
* Communities are encouraged to work together to provide **child care options for all who need assistance and delivery services of meals** and other necessities for senior citizens
* Hoosiers who can **donate blood** are encouraged to visit local blood centers. Blood supplies are low. Please follow the guidance at **[www.redcross.org](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=4aa37727e6c04d67a1020805bebfdea8&_e=6KM88w4uGuqfYmeZsIAJX7ooxRTG2eIqAsha2mbEuo52lns5oJuMTDjmhjHjH-NIssG9sGnf_rQmXobUA-rcaZKmxuhPag4idFeYAc1uXW6_IipmZwZ945TK3nI4Tx6eIGiD702gck6p4QfQLwE5hiemHz3kPrchnRIAAoHLued7zaAUb9s6eCg50Jat4acayv4stWZIpD5mMvOD2UIZzdB-AuGmPeNy5pIOun-nBLCxkXcXf9Xxc-YI6VowKpOtEW1LHdmr-1hLtZGhjxSdDA%3D%3D)**

This comes as the latest developments, following Governor Holcomb’s actions last week, including:
* Signed an executive order **lifting regulations on commercial vehicles transporting goods to retailers --** with the goal of getting more needed items on store shelves
* School corporations will be provided with a **20-day waiver of the required 180 instructional days** for use as needed for the remainder of the academic year.
* State officials are collaborating with the Indiana Department of Education to discuss **solutions regarding student assessments and meals for children** whose schools have closed.
* State agencies are **evaluating rules and regulations** that should be suspended or modified to assist Hoosiers.
* Hoosiers who need to renew their Medicaid eligibility will get **more time to complete the process.** No services will be interrupted.
* Family Social Services Administration (FSSA) has asked federal officials to approve a request to **temporarily waive the renewal process for Hoosiers who need SNAP or TANF benefits.**
* Hoosiers on Medicaid **will not pay co-pays for COVID-19 testing and can get 90-day refills** of medication for chronic conditions.
* FSSA has given **daycares specific guidance to protect children in their care.** Child care and adult day care facilities should institute social distancing and minimize large gatherings. Temporary suspension of operations should be done in consultation with the Family and Social Services and ISDH in the instance of documented community spread.
* The Indiana Department of Correction has **suspended visitation at all facilities.**

As always, please continue to visit the **[Indiana State Department of Health’s coronavirus website](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=4aa37727e6c04d67a1020805bebfdea8&_e=6KM88w4uGuqfYmeZsIAJX2PcZx9DL1ghl7TWIn5AZjNRBTIdy-9AQ9vWr66BL5Qlra2bOfXSyBh0jUgzMg-u-Ly3Tz1LhyuMzU0BlEw3UrP5Us_Zr2RysqDBPkM-85K8b2gbFbPE2AXxVDqO7PtDh7sc1xAhX3VxY2iHg190EnJh8w52tqcxdAblIKquxPlaH1rzHOvu_d2OwztHbdg0BbTvMiQCgXqpagJBK2q9d0NgdKH6o90cKaWRZIYVVhz7YGlRGbBr2O7O_sy5TkpiNQ%3D%3D)** for the latest updates.

**Governor Holcomb is committed to Putting People First – with the health and well being of all Hoosiers being his top priority.**

#
We have a very real public health crisis on our hands, and our representation in IL-16 isn’t cutting it.

Over the weekend, Rep. Adam Kinzinger tweeted, “When this is over, China must be held accountable...Communism fails every time.”

This is what we are up against. In a moment when even the most loyalist members of the GOP are desperately calling for “unity” (they mean grace), in IL-16, we still have Adam Kinzinger. Tone-deaf as usual, encouraging already-scared constituents to turn their fear into condemnation and hatred.

He could be helping, but he’s not.

**Here’s my promise to you _: I will do better._** In moments of crisis, I vow to be a voice of calm and reason. I promise to listen and respond, to work diligently toward solutions to our problems and not create new ones or use old ones as a distraction.

But you know that to get there, I need your help. Tomorrow is our primary, and that means it’s the deadline for pre-primary fundraising.[ Help get us over the line with a $10, $25, or $50 donation. Every bit counts.](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=c305344c35&e=140a9b81e7)

We need new representation in IL-16. Kinzinger is no leader.

Peter, with the Coronavirus making its way through our country it's important to stay up to date on all the latest health measures to keep you and your family safe.

_Here are the latest tips and resources:_

* **SOCIAL DISTANCING** is still recommended.

According to experts at the CDC social distancing can help slow the spread of the Coronavirus in affected communities. This means avoiding crowded places and maintaining distance from others.
* **STAY AT HOME** if you are at risk.

Older adults and people with severe chronic medical conditions are thought to be at higher risk for a severe reaction to the COVID-19. If you or a loved one have conditions that increase risk of getting sick, stay home when possible.
* **IF YOU DEVELOP SYMPTOMS** call your doctor.

Call your doctor if you develop symptoms, have been in close contact with a person known to have Coronavirus, or have recently traveled from an area with widespread or ongoing infection rates.
* **STAY INFORMED by visiting this link:[https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=c193fa4e85bc4b4a91f9fbc15fa90c9c&_e=SR4HyAuUgY_T4lEy2wDiHJobHz0xKiRtWgAxmYTvg4ycw7L-Chyvs0WUlGFxcTrG8ECCkiBnmXnx5EQq1g56VMXiT33f_e6duETW1CRp8iIy7mOX7yByjt-m_GCgw6YWAEsr6z-n_v6HQ3o9FAi5JtGs2z0FXtop4q2Ld5tabJzrIffthQdrubXYmvzyve4LQpZYZ3h9ql8LuGGczvVuvz3gF2482ZAZb39_NCymJ_H3qmV7qZ7V892uUpfflTK6j3rerB2Ft8Hv6MQP38yaY-6_fJUuQdgjyiBhkJX8vnQs1zMJlIEdsZdkCDF6MnzDKtn6Yfxd1KNe-a1X4kxLVVEP_cktP_gKpaFrFVZyNW6dxLbko7c_IQPEFGIglO71IWpAxCZFe2metEHDMwR97CRizYm8eWYbHQVBHMKSkp_QugrHLf1Qtcany8Oa9Anp9xiNTYlPnEA5yTBLLpVsjw%3D%3D)**

The CDC is constantly updating the public with new and vital information regarding the virus.

Stay safe out there, Peter. Together, we will all get through this.

–Team Claudia

| | Hi Crew, As you are well aware, COVID-19 has affected our everyday lives to an extreme degree. Mass gatherings should be avoided during pandemics, so we are restricting our physical interactions in favor of a more virtual presence. Similarly, I agree with the state party's decision to postpone the county conventions next weekend. But, this election has serious ramifications, so we need to reach as many people possible to keep pressing our case. That means, we're counting on our grassroots leaders now more than ever. I'd like you to join our virtual campaign. I need to stay in touch with Iowans, even though I may not be able to visit with you in your community. Here's how you can help: [_**Join our virtual town hall: Click here, RSVP and send me your first question:**_](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f2480/33365068/4a6a7844/3f8beb6a/268446405/VEsH/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "http://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/s/virtual-county-convention-rsvp") | [RSVP >>](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f2480/33365068/4a6a7844/3f8beb6b/268446405/VEsE/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/s/virtual-county-convention-rsvp")
---
We are also launching a social media hub for our supporters. In this Facebook group, you can learn about opportunities to connect with your friends and neighbors about the campaign and not risk COVID-19 exposure.
My team will be engaging here regularly to give you updates on our campaign and to hear what matters most to you. And more importantly , you'll be able to connect to other supporters from all over Iowa.
[Join the Facebook Group >>](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f2480/33365068/4a6a7844/3f8beb64/268446405/VEsF/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "https://www.facebook.com/groups/iowansforfranken/")
---
Thank you, and see you online!
-MTF

name: Jason Yates
subject: VIDEO: National Prayer Service Replay & Prayer Guides
body:
| | Peter, Yesterday, in response to the President’s Day of Prayer Proclamation, we took time to pray across the nation. Tens of thousands participated with My Faith Votes in our online service, _America, It’s Time to Pray_ , from World Outreach Church in Tennessee with Pastor Allen Jackson. The response was so enormous that it literally took down our internet servers for a short period of time! **Whether you had technical difficulties watching the event or simply missed it, I want you to know the event is now available to watch using the link here:[https://www.myfaithvotes.org/national-day-of-prayer-service](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iVl2bNHZpIGlNoQAduC0VVdQwUylQtNErWARJjql45DSJvJTIGXsJR_PPsFAZMg1cQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200316com01&n=1)** Pastor Allen Jackson interviewed Pastor Rob Morgan discussing Biblical lessons from critical moments in our nation’s history. It was a powerful time of worship with fantastic teaching. It’s undoubtedly a service you will want to take time to watch. **We’ve also made available two FREE resources for you to use during these unprecedented times: our[COVID-19 Pandemic Prayer Guide](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iVl2bNHZpIGlNoQAduC0VVdQwUylQtNErWARJjql45DSJvJTIGXsJR_PPsFAZMg1cQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200316com01&n=2) and [America, It’s Time to Pray](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iVl2bNHZpIGlNoQAduC0VVdQwUylQtNErWARJjql45DSJvJTIGXsJR_PPsFAZMg1cQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200316com01&n=3), an e-book adapted from Robert Morgan’s book, _100 Bible Verses That Made America, Defining Moments that Shaped Our Enduring Foundations of Faith_.** [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iVl2bNHZpIGlNoQAduC0VVdQwUylQtNErWARJjql45DSJvJTIGXsJR_PPsFAZMg1cQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200316com01&n=4) We are standing with you in prayer for our families, communities, and nation. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO

Friends \--

As we all adjust our lives to mitigate the spread of COVID-19, I understand that there is a lot of uncertainty. But I want you to know that I am doing everything in my power as Council President to keep San Diego families safe and prevent this crisis from getting worse.

**Tomorrow, I'm calling for an emergency City Council meeting to officially ratify the Mayor’s State of Emergency Declaration in San Diego. This is an important step, but we must also act immediately to protect those facing economic turmoil as a result of this crisis. That’s why I have also proposed additional steps to protect our residents.**

Some of the measures I am proposing include: \- An immediate freeze on residential evictions due to nonpayment
\- A freeze on mortgage foreclosures on Housing Authority properties
\- A freeze on small business evictions from commercial leases
\- Providing relief to individuals and landlords suffering from loss of income
\- Ensure that we enforce paid sick leave and minimum wage ordinances
\- Stopping all utility shut-offs due to nonpayment
\- A halt on ICE operations in sensitive areas such as hospitals and clinics. This is a difficult time, but I am so proud of the San Diego residents who have done their part to help mitigate the spread of this virus. Social distancing, practicing good hygiene, staying home — it’s a shared effort that requires everyone’s buy-in.

I will continue to monitor this situation closely, but in the meantime, I would like to direct your attention to the **[San Diego COVID-19 Community Response Fund](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16171684/171486794/1510772273?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MGNkZjkyOC1mNTY3LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0szcQy_BVOpWBep6ankjJ2jSCSRQp3yIlxvaauttjk8=)** , which is deploying resources to community-based organizations at the frontlines of the coronavirus outbreak in San Diego.

This fund is focused on three areas: food security, rental and utility assistance, and income replacement or gap funding. **[If you can, please consider making a contribution to support this urgent work>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16171685/171486795/-614052814?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODk3NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MGNkZjkyOC1mNTY3LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0szcQy_BVOpWBep6ankjJ2jSCSRQp3yIlxvaauttjk8=)**

More soon,

_Georgette Gómez_

body:
This afternoon, President Trump and the White House Coronavirus Task Force issued new guidelines to help protect Americans during... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=12395e8cbd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 16, 2020
---
| | |
# **15 days to slow the spread**

**This afternoon,** President Trump and the White House Coronavirus Task Force issued new guidelines to help protect Americans during the global Coronavirus outbreak.

**🎬****President Trump:** [Taking action now will keep America ahead of the curve](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f4638444fa&e=3ee4985eea)

**The new recommendations** are simple to follow but will have a resounding impact on public health. While the President leads a nationwide response, bringing together government resources and private-sector ingenuity, every American can help slow the virus’ spread and keep our most high-risk populations safe:
1. Listen to and follow the directions of your**state and local authorities.**

2. **If you feel sick, stay home**. Do not go to work. Contact your medical provider.

3. **If your children are sick** , keep them at home. Contact your medical provider.

4. If someone in your household **has tested positive for the Coronavirus** , keep the entire household at home.

5. **If you are an older American** , stay home and away from other people.

6. If you are a person with a **serious underlying health condition** —such as a significant heart or lung disease—stay home and away from other people.

[**Today’s guidelines**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=07a7cb5de7&e=3ee4985eea) build on the CDC’s recommendations to help prevent spread of the virus. Americans should continue practicing strict personal hygiene, including washing hands regularly for at least 20 seconds at a time and wiping down surfaces in the home regularly.

**Even if you are young** and otherwise healthy, you are at risk—and your activities can increase the risk of contracting the Coronavirus for others. Everyone can do their part.

**There’s no better way** to protect your family and your community than by arming yourself with [accurate, up-to-date information](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e8f915f77d&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
|
## **A massive, unprecedented nationwide response**

**President Trump** declared a National Emergency in response to the Coronavirus on Friday, freeing up more than $42 billion in resources for states and localities.

**The emergency declaration** will give healthcare providers on the front lines of this pandemic the flexibility they need to respond. President Trump continues to cut through every piece of unnecessary Washington red tape that may hinder response efforts, and he is continuing to make every Federal resource available to those who need it.

**“I know I speak** on behalf of the President and our entire team when I say how grateful we are for governors all across the country and the seamless partnership that we have forged with them and with state health officials,” Vice President Mike Pence said yesterday.

**“We also want to express** great appreciation to the American people. Not surprisingly, it is inspiring to see the way tens of millions of Americans are responding with compassion [and] with common sense.”

[Kay Coles James: President Trump’s declaration helps mobilize our nation](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9ebda5572b&e=3ee4985eea)

name: Alex Meyer
subject: We’re in this together.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scheller for Congress, Inc
body:
I hope that you and your family are well during these trying times. As Pennsylvania, the United States, and the world confront the coronavirus pandemi Peter, I hope that you and your family are well during these trying times. As Pennsylvania, the United States, and the world confront the coronavirus pandemic,**Lisa has evaluated the situation and decided to suspend any in-person events going forward. **
Lisa wanted you to know that this decision **does not** come lightly. She is committed to meeting all members of our Lehigh Valley community and sharing her ideas for our country’s future – but now is the time to focus on the present danger we face.
Lisa has made it clear; we need to watch out for each other, help protect the most vulnerable groups – anyone immunocompromised or our senior citizens – because ultimately **we are all in this together**.
Lisa has asked me, to ask two things from you today, Peter:
First and foremost: Please wash your hands. Cover your coughs. Practice social distancing. Do what you can to make sure that you, your family, and your neighbors remain as healthy as possible.
Second, our campaign is still pushing forward, Peter. While we are not meeting face-to-face with voters, our efforts to spread the word about the problems our district faces and solutions Lisa wants to bring to Washington must continue.
[**That is why we are asking supporters to consider making calls for our campaign from your homes.**** **](http://links.schellerforcongress.com/u/click?_t=2618b931a0124f90ba4a51cf9fda2c48&_m=e1e5a56cfae74491b335fe8878e98220&_e=u-tCVtnaBgrcZqUT1iTJgn3nztz_zpJZdLNCOVYDbqlNxbRQq5J_hxSxRfQ2v2JWdbLt_vNeJ8yROB1n850tj8S6LLkWF2a5x5a8FkWialCH8mpsyFvSOFo1uMrZIS6f1jncCxIUTtqwlY1Pa3yWO57pkNKYN7-SggjhEJdPFm7TcfJoSE95l_BUGaLU6_OgCJIRgfMMmuiPAWjqIM_pe0h-8PUIHcI5cGLNvJrDqxR7FpArSwyeYugECbzDM_jJvymj7Y2uAziXyreKHKk-h1ErPcS281qTXHZjcDdo477SPtScdiPD06KXrixtQmBTCCRV-fNjYEIY1S8Xrn5JTPwyUakVto4LhgenP2HRPP4c6vDuOVeTBOAlqZdlwWev8Xo_Gv5VCVnTHJFCdSLURBVcxMiTjDV7e2skh_zMNok%3D)
As we near election day, instead of door knocking, attending events, or donating money – we’re asking you to talk to your neighbors about Lisa’s candidacy. **[Please take a few minutes out of your day to help get the word out about Lisa and her plans for the Lehigh Valley.](http://links.schellerforcongress.com/u/click?_t=2618b931a0124f90ba4a51cf9fda2c48&_m=e1e5a56cfae74491b335fe8878e98220&_e=u-tCVtnaBgrcZqUT1iTJgn3nztz_zpJZdLNCOVYDbqlNxbRQq5J_hxSxRfQ2v2JWdbLt_vNeJ8yROB1n850tj8S6LLkWF2a5x5a8FkWialCH8mpsyFvSOFo1uMrZIS6f1jncCxIUTtqwlY1Pa3yWO57pkNKYN7-SggjhEJdPFm7TcfJoSE95l_BUGaLU6_Oge6PoZBz3p2NasIE86ODOkEE_ss4hX4XifL1e5ZswwCazwnGTWPPuzlV3vkOaaX-KU4r6zDNOvF78oFCusG00pcrgk0lrYOSrw1AeYL6JHisqKXX0Gvbu5TdKmcHSAqA7OHnayh7sDminB3OzIrHckAgiTXHeeRCUgMOiW5iyLuV41csBL1ItBIAAi-1VaAzSoHCzcTkmGTdqT6eNLBobYF7ZXvplk-IQ50FePVMJwYw%3D)**
Thank you for your continued support and the energy you’re putting behind Lisa and our campaign. She is ready to fight for Pennsylvania and our Lehigh Valley community, and looks forward to continuing to promote her ideas once we beat this virus once and for all.
[**We hope you will consider contributing your time to elect conservative Lisa Scheller to Congress**.](http://links.schellerforcongress.com/u/click?_t=2618b931a0124f90ba4a51cf9fda2c48&_m=e1e5a56cfae74491b335fe8878e98220&_e=u-tCVtnaBgrcZqUT1iTJgn3nztz_zpJZdLNCOVYDbqlNxbRQq5J_hxSxRfQ2v2JWdbLt_vNeJ8yROB1n850tj8S6LLkWF2a5x5a8FkWialCH8mpsyFvSOFo1uMrZIS6f1jncCxIUTtqwlY1Pa3yWO57pkNKYN7-SggjhEJdPFm7TcfJoSE95l_BUGaLU6_Og0vpnUMIROiHkFgICwEqAYRca0x9KTA9R8FOorCUsyQthdlSV6tQ_Sed1GAMt9wPgVsutZ1FMXxPGJgBgpOQEl8MPIMNPyAFtChU1jYVdn5cEUDrrCUJlUlR1sJ6_4vJJRUiOoA39otD-eX0iz3L_rOOIXtuQnsyyO7wgwpOaRz31f5h4zWTrh-tHUaj6IrbMFccTxtl85T_QGF6T7Ht4KVQJE6-Gzn0kJU_-s1ggWcc%3D)
All the best,
Alex Meyer Lisa Scheller for Congress Campaign Manager Paid for by Scheller for Congress, Inc. name: Joe Kennedy III
subject: How we can help those most in need
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kennedy for Massachusetts
body:
| [](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390029/55b66746/232bc791/80e2e9b/158881512/VEsH/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "http://www.kennedyforma.com/")
---

| | I hope you and your family are holding up ok. I know it is incredibly disorienting, uncertain, and scary right now. And a lot of us feel helpless - particularly as we're stuck at home, unable to physically be with neighbors and friends who may be suffering or struggling. But here's one, simple thing we can all do: Donate to the frontline organizations doing the hard work of protecting the most vulnerable in the face of this crisis. It doesn't have to be a lot - just a few dollars can make all the difference to the groups trying to ensure that no one goes hungry, without a roof over their head, or without access to basic health care during this global epidemic. As most of you know, we temporarily shut down campaign activities in the midst of this crisis - that includes fundraising for ourselves online. Instead, we are going to leverage the power of this email list to help those most in need. We've put together a list of some of the organizations - in Massachusetts and across the country - doing this incredible work. Read about them below and click DONATE to chip in where you can. **Donate to Project Bread, Pine Street Inn, and the Food Bank of Western Mass** Ensure those in Massachusetts who are homeless or in need have the food and assistance they need: [Donate](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390029/55b66746/232bc791/80e2ea4/158881512/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiI3In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-covidma?refcode=2020-03-16-charities&recurring=1&amount=7") **Donate to the United Way Central Mass and United Way Mass Bay** Help ensure United Ways in Massachusetts have the resources they need to help vulnerable communities: [Donate](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390029/55b66746/232bc791/80e2ea5/158881512/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiI3In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-unitedway?refcode=2020-03-16-charities&recurring=1&amount=7") **Donate to Meals on Wheels and Feeding America** Ensure the elderly and the hungry across the country do not go starving in the midst of this epidemic: [Donate](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390029/55b66746/232bc791/80e2ea6/158881512/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiI3In0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-covid?refcode=2020-03-16-charities&recurring=1&amount=7") I am working to do everything I can on Capitol Hill to provide support during this crisis. But organizations like the ones above are on the front lines, delivering immediate assistance where it is most needed. Let's show them we stand with them. Let's put the full force of this community into helping each other get through these difficult times. I'm grateful. More soon,
**NOTE:** We have temporarily suspended all political activities in the wake of the COVID-19. For more information visit **[KennedyForMA.com/COVID19](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/36390029/55b66746/232bc791/80e2ea7/158881512/VEsD/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://www.kennedyforma.com/covid19/")** **INFORMATION ABOUT DONATIONS****:** All donations to the links above will be contributed directly to the charities mentioned. Kennedy For MA receives no financial benefit from these donations. Project Bread works to prevent and end hunger in Massachusetts, providing access to food to hungry people today and working to break the cycle of hunger. ProjectBread.org Pine Street Inn provides a comprehensive range of services to nearly 2,000 homeless men and women each day. PineStreetInn.org The Food Bank of Western Massachusetts provides food to independent pantries, meal sites, and shelters that are on the front lines of emergency food assistance. FoodBankWMA.org United Way of Central Massachusetts has created the "We Care Fund", a new emergency fund to respond to the COVID-19 crisis by helping those hardest hit in our community. UnitedWayCM.org United Way of Massachusetts Bay and Merrimack Valley has established the "COVID-19 Family Support Fund" to provide flexible cash assistance to help families weather the COVID-19 crisis. UnitedWayMassBay.org Meals on Wheels America supports more than 5000 community-based programs across America that deliver nutritious meals, friendly visits, and safety checks that enable seniors to live nourished lives with independence and dignity. MealsOnWheelsAmerica.org Feeding America feeds America's hungry through a nationwide network of food banks. FeedingAmerica.org |
